tools.name: browse
tools.description: Fetch a web page by URL and return its readable content as Markdown (or HTML). Use this to read the full content of a specific page — for example a URL the user gives you, or a result you found via web search — when a search snippet is not enough to answer. For pages that build their content in the browser (search results, dashboards, single-page apps), use the wait_* parameters so the dynamically loaded content is captured.

url.description: The full URL of the page to read, including http:// or https://.

format.description: Return the content as 'markdown' (default, readable) or 'html' (raw).

wait_until.description: When to capture the page. 'networkidle' (default) captures when the network goes quiet — good for most pages. 'done' waits until ALL page operations finish (slower, fullest render). 'networkalmostidle' tolerates a couple of lingering connections (keep-alive/analytics). 'load' / 'domcontentloaded' fire earliest. This is the lever for how long to wait — wait_ms is only the deadline, not a delay.

wait_selector.description: Optional CSS selector to wait for before capturing the page. The most precise way to wait for specific client-side-rendered content — capture happens once this element appears (or wait_ms elapses).

wait_ms.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the wait_until / wait_selector condition before giving up and capturing whatever is there (a deadline/timeout, NOT a fixed delay — there is no fixed-sleep mode). Default 5000, max 30000. Raise it for slow pages.

configure.name: configure
configure.title: Web access policy
configure.description: Choose which sites this tool is allowed to open. Required once before the tool can serve; it re-locks on every restart, so set it again after a redeploy.

allowed_domains.description: Comma-separated domains (subdomains included), or * for any.

x-privasys.label: Allowed domains
x-privasys.details: A safety policy that stops the tool being used to reach arbitrary internal or unintended hosts. Until you set it, the tool stays locked and returns 'awaiting initial configuration'.

store.tagline: Let your AI assistant read web pages, inside a confidential enclave.
store.keywords: web page, fetch, read url, browse, markdown, web content, ai tool, mcp, confidential computing, attested, enclave
store.description:
Web Page Reader gives an AI assistant the ability to open a specific web page and return its main content as clean Markdown (or raw HTML), so it can read and reason over the full text of a page rather than just a search snippet — useful for following a link the user shared, or digging into a result found via web search.

The page is fetched and rendered entirely inside a confidential enclave: the URL and the retrieved content are processed in hardware-isolated, attestable memory. The owner configures, once at deploy time, which domains the tool may reach, so it can never be turned into an open proxy to unintended hosts. Verifying clients can confirm exactly which build is running before they trust its output.

Add it to an AI assistant alongside web search to go from 'here are some links' to 'here is what the page actually says.'
